prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='FT_catboost', seasonality_champion='NaiveSeasonal', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.848) and very high seasonality variance (1.0579), indicating models capture patterns very differently. FT_catboost leads trend tracking while NaiveSeasonal dominates overall RMSE and seasonality capture. The combination of high disagreement and seasonality variance strongly supports DBA combination method to leverage complementary strengths across the model ensemble.
prompts.skeptic:

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "FT_catboost",
  "seasonality_champion": "NaiveSeasonal",
  "overall_champion": "NaiveSeasonal",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "late": "NaiveSeasonal"},
  "tier1_models": ["NaiveSeasonal", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "ARIMA", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "CWT_rf", "CWT_catboost", "DWT_catboost", "DWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_CWT_catboost", "FT_rf", "svr", "rf", "ONLY_FT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_svr", "FT_catboost", "NaiveMovingAverage"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.848,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "down", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.848) and very high seasonality variance (1.0579), indicating models capture patterns very differently. FT_catboost leads trend tracking while NaiveSeasonal dominates overall RMSE and seasonality capture. The combination of high disagreement and seasonality variance strongly supports DBA combination method to leverage complementary strengths across the model ensemble."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35": {"top_k": 5, "shrinkage": 0.35}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": true,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=6 indicates strong per-horizon model diversity, favoring selection methods like best_per_horizon_by_validation. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination due to high seasonality variance (1.0579) and trend/seasonality champion complementarity. With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 is appropriate for stability. inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 adds weighted diversity with shrinkage. RMSE spread ratio=0.848 justifies rmse_focus preset. Force debate because DBA and selection methods may be within 2% of each other given the heterogeneous horizon patterns."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current set has 4 candidates with diverse types: selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), baseline (dba_combination), weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). No leakage detected - all use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection. Universe top candidates (inverse_rmse_weights_k2_*) have lower scores (~0.74) than current best (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 at 0.77). Score margin between top2 is 0.028 which is acceptable. No redundancy issues - each candidate serves different purpose. n_windows=3 meets minimum for weighted candidates with learns_weights=true.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.848) and very high seasonality variance (1.0579), indicating models capture patterns very differently. FT_catboost leads trend tracking while NaiveSeasonal dominates overall RMSE and seasonality capture. The combination of high disagreement and seasonality variance strongly supports DBA combination method to leverage complementary strengths across the model ensemble.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=6 indicates strong per-horizon model diversity, favoring selection methods like best_per_horizon_by_validation. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination due to high seasonality variance (1.0579) and trend/seasonality champion complementarity. With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 is appropriate for stability. inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 adds weighted diversity with shrinkage. RMSE spread ratio=0.848 justifies rmse_focus preset. Force debate because DBA and selection methods may be within 2% of each other given the heterogeneous horizon patterns.

skeptic.rationale: Current set has 4 candidates with diverse types: selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), baseline (dba_combination), weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35). No leakage detected - all use anti-leakage rolling/expanding selection. Universe top candidates (inverse_rmse_weights_k2_*) have lower scores (~0.74) than current best (topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 at 0.77). Score margin between top2 is 0.028 which is acceptable. No redundancy issues - each candidate serves different purpose. n_windows=3 meets minimum for weighted candidates with learns_weights=true.
